element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Jump height enhancement, levitation, underwater breathing, lava walking, force field passage

element_introduction_order.element: Ramses mummy and King Ramses spirit guidance

typical_tension_triggers: Final mothership timer (MS-DOS: 15 minutes to disarm bomb)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Metroidvania-influenced progression with gated exploration through artifact acquisition, creating cycles of restriction and capability opening

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Radio transmission device pieces scattered throughout world; collection determines ending outcome
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Previously unreachable areas become accessible after obtaining corresponding ancient artifacts (traversal-gating mechanic)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to Kilmaat and basic minions; standard FPS combat patterns
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Expansion of enemy types (scorpions, evil spirits); Set boss encounter; artifact acquisition begins opening new areas
difficulty_curve.late_game: Culmination in Ramses mummy recovery; console versions feature endgame state dependent on collection completeness; MS-DOS includes mothership infiltration with time pressure
notable_spikes: MS-DOS: Kilmaat mothership 15-minute nuclear disarm timer

notable_level_patterns.description: Game world connected via overworld map with areas locked behind artifact abilities (jump height, levitation, underwater breathing, lava walking, force field passage). Encourages backtracking and re-exploration.
Mid-game Set boss encounter serves as major progression gate; console versions branch endings based on prior collectible completion
MS-DOS version uniquely features mothership level with 15-minute armed nuclear weapon timer creating escalating tension
Eight radio transmission pieces distributed throughout world; completion determines which of two endings player receives
